description: The bird was a chunky heron with a thick, dagger-like bill perched in a fallen tree with numerous branches preventing a clear view. On the first night, the bird sat with its head pulled down into its shoulders, preventing a good, clean look at the face, but we could see heavy black on the side of the face and a white or yellow head plume (exact color was difficult to assess in the dusk light). The bird had a streaked gray back typical of an adult Yellow-crowned Night-Heron. The bird did not move noticeably during our observation, which consisted mainly of moving from one vantage point to another trying to get the best looks and photographs possible. The next morning Derek went back to check on the bird’s continued presence and got a clear look at the bird’s head, including the yellow crown above a thick black eyestripe that met with a black chinstrap at the back of the head, leaving a small white stripe under the eye. No vocalizations were heard.
photographer: Photographer: Shelley Rutkin, Derek Hudgins, Derek Hudgins
